raw_items.title: Catholics in Latin America generally have positive views of Pope Leo
raw_items.summary: In six Latin American countries surveyed, most Catholics view Pope Leo XIV favorably, though his ratings trail those Pope Francis received early in his papacy.

raw_items.title: Most U.S. Catholics view Pope Leo favorably; many think Trump has been too critical of him
raw_items.summary: About eight-in-ten U.S. Catholics view Pope Leo XIV favorably, though opinions on the tension between Leo and President Trump split sharply along party lines.

raw_items.title: Is U.S. fertility at an all-time low? It depends how you measure it
raw_items.summary: U.S. fertility rates have hit historic lows, but three common measures tell different stories about whether American families are truly shrinking.

raw_items.title: What the data says about abortion in the U.S.
raw_items.summary: Six-in-ten US adults say abortion should be legal in all or most cases. Here's a look at data on abortion rates, providers, demographics and more.

raw_items.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue strikes despite ceasefire agreement
raw_items.summary: Several people are reported killed in southern Lebanon, with the IDF saying it hit "Hezbollah terrorist targets" after the group fired over 50 projectiles at Israeli forces.

raw_items.title: Zelensky stripped of highest Polish honour over WW2 name of army unit
raw_items.summary: Ukraine has denounced the move, calling it a "strategic mistake" and "disrespectful".

raw_items.title: Tourist dies in Dominican Republic luxury resort fire
raw_items.summary: Nearly 1,700 people were evacuated from the huge blaze at Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach in Bayahibe, a popular resort town on the Caribbean coast.

raw_items.title: Italy's Meloni says Trump 'made up' story that she 'begged' him for photo at G7
raw_items.summary: The highly public exchange is an indication that their earlier close ties have frayed  since Trump's decision to go to war with Iran.
